title: Non-record: Skill Forge — Autonomous ML Experimentation System (Local RTX 4070)
body:
## Summary

Non-record submission demonstrating **Skill Forge**, an autonomous ML experimentation system for Parameter Golf.

- **Approach**: Self-evolving skill system built with Claude Code — skills start as research-backed heuristics and crystallize into specific playbooks as experiments prove patterns
- **Validation**: RTX 4070 Laptop (8GB VRAM), 7L/512d model, val_bpb=1.8990 (local, not competition-comparable)
- **Compute grant pending**: 8×H100 competition runs planned once awarded

## Why This Is Interesting

Rather than manually tuning experiments, Skill Forge runs an autoresearch-style loop (inspired by [Karpathy autoresearch](https://github.com/karpathy/autoresearch)) where:
1. An exploration policy selects which domain skill to consult
2. The domain skill proposes a structured hypothesis
3. The experiment runner modifies `train_gpt.py`, trains, measures, keeps/discards
4. Every 5 experiments, a meta-layer evaluates skill effectiveness and evolves them

**Each individual experiment runs within the 10-minute budget on 8xH100.** The system automates the researcher outer loop — proposing hypotheses, testing them (each test is one compliant 10-minute run), and learning from results.

The domain skills encode knowledge from analysis of all 21 leaderboard submissions and 13+ recent arXiv papers (Feb-March 2026), including XSA, 6 Muon optimizer variants, GPTQ-lite implementation details, and quantization gap measurements.

## Compute Constraints

Validated locally on an RTX 4070 Laptop (8GB VRAM) by:
- Scaling model to 7L/512d (same weight matrices as competition 11L/512d)
- Reducing seq_len to 512 (vs 2048 competition)
- Adding SKIP_QUANT_EVAL to avoid post-training OOM
- Using math SDPA (FlashAttention not available on Ada Lovelace)

Techniques validated at 512d width transfer to competition dimensions — relative rankings hold.

## Files

| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| Flynns-Approach-README.md | Full methodology, system architecture, research sources |
| records/.../README.md | Detailed submission writeup with results table |
| records/.../submission.json | Metadata |
| records/.../train_gpt.py | Training script (3 backward-compatible mods for local GPU) |
| records/.../train_local_rtx4070.log | Full training log |

## Next Steps (After Compute Grant)

Each step below produces a single compliant run (10 min or less on 8xH100, 16MB or less artifact):

1. **Validate baseline**: Single run of unmodified train_gpt.py on 8xH100 (about 1.22 BPB — this is the starting point, not our target)
2. **Apply locally-proven techniques**: Architecture scaling (11L, 3x MLP, XSA), optimizer tuning (EMA, Muon momentum 0.99, WD=0.04), and compression (int6 QAT, GPTQ-lite) — each validated by a separate compliant 10-minute run
3. **Iterate with Skill Forge**: The system proposes and tests hypotheses — each test is one compliant 10-minute training run. Multiple iterations find the best configuration.
4. **Submit best result**: 3-seed validation of the winning configuration for statistical significance. Target: beat current SOTA (1.1228 BPB) by at least 0.005 nats.
